concerns.text: FR-001 requires downloading and caching the full eBird Basic Dataset (EBD) and NOAA/PRISM for 2020–2024. The plan (Summary, Technical Context) explicitly states it will use 'sampled data subsets' and 'proxy datasets' (HuggingFace samples) because the full data exceeds CI limits. There is no plan element describing how the system will satisfy the requirement to download/cache the *full* dataset, nor is there a mechanism to switch to full data in production. The plan effectively admits the FR cannot be met as written.

FR-005 mandates 'permutation tests (10,000 shuffles)'. The plan (Compute Feasibility) notes this will be 'parallelized across the 2 cores' but does not address the runtime feasibility. Running 10,000 shuffles on a CPU-only 2-core CI runner for multiple species/grid cells is likely to exceed the 6-hour runtime limit (SC-005) or the 7GB RAM limit. The plan lacks a strategy (e.g., reducing shuffle count, early stopping, or subsampling) to ensure this specific FR is achievable within the stated constraints.

FR-006 requires 'Riemannian manifold' trajectory analysis. The plan mentions 'Riemannian trajectory analysis' in the Summary and Complexity Tracking, but the 'Technical Context' and 'Dependencies' list standard stats libraries (`statsmodels`, `pygam`, `scikit-learn`) which do not natively support Riemannian manifold statistics. No specific library (e.g., `geomstats`, `pymanopt`) or implementation strategy is identified to satisfy this complex mathematical requirement.

SC-005 requires the total pipeline runtime to be within the CI runner time limit. The plan sets a goal of '≤ 6 hours' but the combination of full GAMM fitting, 10,000 permutation shuffles (FR-005), and Riemannian analysis (FR-006) on even a sampled dataset is highly likely to exceed this. The plan relies on 'aggressive data filtering' but does not quantify the sample size reduction needed to guarantee SC-005 is met, leaving the success criterion untestable.
